Explanation: The question describes a moss with a yellow-green color, low growth, and "Threatened" status in 2023, whose genus was named after an Englishman (William Wilson, a bryologist) at the time of his retirement, 73 years after his birth. The genus "Wilsoniella" fits these criteria, as it was named after William Wilson, a prominent English bryologist. The most well-known threatened, yellow-green moss in this genus is Wilsoniella britannica.
Exact Answer: Wilsoniella britannica
